column11: Etymology

安 ān - Anqing
徽 huī - Huizhou (now Huangshan city)
double celebration or celebrate again

福 fú – Fuzhou
建 jiàn – Jianzhou (today's Jian'ou)

甘 gān - Ganzhou District, Zhangye
肃 sù - Suzhou District, Jiuquan

广 guǎng - "Wide"
东 dōng- "East"
Lit. "The Eastern Expanse"
(Guangxi being West)

Abbreviated from "Guangnan Xi Lu" (A "lu" was equal to a province or a state in the Song Dynasty)
广 = wide
西 = west
literally, "The Western Expanse" (Guangdong being East)

贵 guì - Gui Mountains
州 zhōu - zhou (prefecture)

海 hǎi – sea
南 nán – south
"South of the Sea [Qiongzhou Strait]"

河 hé—"(Yellow) River"
北 běi—"north"
"north of the Yellow River"

黑 hēi—black
龙 lóng—dragon
江 jiāng—river
"Amur River"
Official form: 黑龙江省 (-shěng, “province”).

河 hé - (Yellow) River
南 nán - south
"south of the Yellow River"
fragrant harbour

湖 hú - lake
北 běi - north
"north of Lake Dongting"

湖 hú - lake
南 nán - south
"south of the lake "
From the Mongolian öbür monggol, where öbür means the front, sunny side of natural barrier (a mountain, mountain range, lake or desert)

江 jiāng - Jiangning (now Nanjing)
苏 sū - Suzhou

Contraction of:
江南西; Jiāngnán Xī
"The western Jiangnan"
from girin ula, a Manchu phrase meaning "along the river"
Peace forever for the Liao River watershed

Perhaps from the A-Ma Temple (Chinese: 媽閣廟; Mandarin Pinyin: Māgé Miào), a temple built in 1448 dedicated to Matsu – the goddess of seafarers and fishermen.
The present Chinese name (Chinese: 澳門; Mandarin Pinyin: Àomén) means "Inlet Gates".

宁 níng—tranquil
夏 xià—Western Xia
"Tranquil Xia"

From Khoshut Oirat Köknuur:
Köke - blue
Nuur - Lake
"blue lake" (Qinghai Lake)
In Chinese:
青 qīng - cyan
海 hǎi - sea
"qing (blue/green/black) sea"

陕 shǎn - Shanzhou (now Shan County, Henan)
西 xī - west
"west of Shanzhou"

山 shān – mountain
东 dōng  – east
"east of the Taihang Mountains"

上 – above
海 – sea
"upon the sea"

山 shān - mountain
西 xī - west
"west of the Taihang Mountains"

Short for 川峡四路 chuānxiá sìlù
literally "The Four Circuits
of the Rivers and Gorges",
referring to the four circuits during the Song Dynasty

天 – sky
津 – ferry

Disputed (Tibet names in European languages are loanwords from Arabic Tibat or Tobatt and Persian Tubbat)
Official form: 西藏自治区 Xīzàng Zìzhìqū (“autonomous region”).

新 xīn – new
疆 jiāng – frontier
"new frontier"

云 yún – Yunling Mountains
南 nán – south
"South of the Yunling Mountains"

折江 - "crooked river"
Now the river's name is Qiantang River, in honour of the kings of Wuyue

北 - north
京 - capital
"Northern Capital"
